headerDisplay:
Setting up an Air+ cloud device (AC0650 / AC1715)?
Click Plugin Settings from the plugin card menu to open the Air+ Setup Wizard. The wizard saves your token and fills in the UUID automatically — you should not need to edit those fields by hand.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of your air purifier as it will appear in HomeKit

host.title: IP Address or Cloud Placeholder
host.description: IP address of your Philips Air Purifier. Use 'cloud' for airplus-cloud devices.

protocol.title: Protocol
protocol.description: Communication protocol. Use 'http' for AC1xxx DH/AES models, 'homeid-http' for HomeID/Condor local HTTP devices, or 'airplus-cloud' for devices registered in the Philips Air+ app (cloud MQTT).

useHttps.title: Use HTTPS for HomeID
useHttps.description: Use HTTPS for the HomeID local API. Only applies when Protocol is 'homeid-http'.

clientId.title: HomeID Client ID
clientId.description: Base64 HomeID local API client ID. Only required for HomeID devices that return a PhilipsCondor authentication challenge.

clientSecret.title: HomeID Client Secret
clientSecret.description: Base64 HomeID local API client secret. Only required for HomeID devices that return a PhilipsCondor authentication challenge.

encryptionKey.title: HomeID Encryption Key
encryptionKey.description: Optional hex AES key for HomeID local HTTP payload encryption. If omitted and credentials are provided, the plugin attempts to fetch it from the device security endpoint.

airplusDeviceUuid.title: Air+ Device UUID
airplusDeviceUuid.description: Device UUID - filled in automatically by the Air+ Setup Wizard. Only enter manually if you ran scripts/airplus_setup.py from the command line.

airplusTokenFile.title: Air+ Token File Path
airplusTokenFile.description: Path to the token file. Set automatically by the Air+ Setup Wizard. Leave blank to use the default ~/.homebridge/philips-airplus-{uuid}.json location.

pythonPath.title: Python Path (Optional)
pythonPath.description: Path to Python 3.12 or newer with aiocoap and pycryptodomex installed. Leave blank to use the plugin's bundled virtual environment (recommended).
